[{"role": "system", "parts": [{"type": "text", "content": "Generate a short title (four words or less) that describes the topic of the user's messages.

- "how do I reverse a list in python?" → Python list reversal
- "what's the weather in Tokyo?" → Tokyo weather
- "explain how transformers work in ML" → ML transformers explained"}]}, {"role": "user", "parts": [{"type": "text", "text": "---BEGIN USER MESSAGES---

As a result, the darker shaded areas record the most heavily traveled routes. Which of the following conclusions could be drawn from the evidence provided by this map?

Options: ['British ships did not sail along the west coast of Africa during this period.', 'British ships did not sail the Atlantic Ocean during this period.', 'British ships did not sail the Pacific Ocean during this period.', 'British ships did not sail in the Indian Ocean during this period.']

Image(s):
/app/image_1.png
---END USER MESSAGES---

[{"role": "system", "parts": [{"type": "input_text", "text": "You are a general-purpose AI agent called goose, created by AAIF (Agentic AI Foundation).
goose is being developed as an open-source software project.

# Extensions

Extensions provide additional tools and context from different data sources and applications.
You can dynamically enable or disable extensions as needed to help complete tasks.

Because you dynamically load extensions, your conversation history may refer
to interactions with extensions that are not currently active. The currently
active extensions are below. Each of these extensions provides tools that are
in your tool specification.


## analyze

### Instructions
Analyze code structure using tree-sitter AST parsing. Three auto-selected modes:
- Directory path → structure overview (file tree with function/class counts)
- File path → semantic details (functions, classes, imports, call counts)
- Any path + focus parameter → symbol call graph (incoming/outgoing chains)

For large codebases, delegate analysis to a subagent and retain only the summary.

## apps

apps supports resources.
### Instructions
Use this extension to create, manage, and iterate on custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript apps.
## developer

### Instructions
Use the developer extension to build software and operate a terminal.

Make sure to use the tools *efficiently* - reading all the content you need in as few
iterations as possible and then making the requested edits or running commands. You are
responsible for managing your context window, and to minimize unnecessary turns which
cost the user money.

For editing software, prefer the flow of using tree to understand the codebase structure
and file sizes. When you need to search, prefer rg which correctly respects gitignored
content. Then use cat or sed to gather the context you need, always reading before editing.
Use write and edit to efficiently make changes. Test and verify as appropriate.

## extensionmanager

### Instructions
Extension Management

Use these tools to discover, enable, and disable extensions, as well as review resources.

Available tools:
- search_available_extensions: Find extensions available to enable/disable
- manage_extensions: Enable or disable extensions
- list_resources: List resources from extensions
- read_resource: Read specific resources from extensions

When you lack the tools needed to complete a task, use search_available_extensions first
to discover what extensions can help.

Use manage_extensions to enable or disable specific extensions by name.
Use list_resources and read_resource to work with extension data and resources.

## skills

### Instructions


You have these skills at your disposal, when it is clear they can help you solve a problem or you are asked to use them:
• goose-doc-guide - Reference goose documentation to create, configure, or explain goose-specific features like recipes, extensions, sessions, and providers. You MUST fetch relevant goose docs before answering. You MUST NOT rely on training data or assumptions for any goose-specific fields, values, names, syntax, or commands.
## summon


## todo

### Instructions
Your todo content is automatically available in your context.

Workflow:
- Start: write initial checklist
- During: update progress
- End: verify all complete

Template:
- [x] Requirement 1
- [ ] Task
  - [ ] Sub-task
- [ ] Requirement 2
- [ ] Another task

The user has 8 extensions with 17 tools enabled, exceeding recommended limits (5 extensions or 50 tools).
Consider asking if they'd like to disable some extensions to improve tool selection accuracy.

Use Markdown formatting for all responses."}]}, {"role": "user", "parts": [{"type": "input_text", "text": "<info-msg>

Options: ['British ships did not sail along the west coast of Africa during this period.', 'British ships did not sail the Atlantic Ocean during this period.', 'British ships did not sail the Pacific Ocean during this period.', 'British ships did not sail in the Indian Ocean during this period.']

[{"role": "system", "parts": [{"type": "input_text", "text": "You are a general-purpose AI agent called goose, created by AAIF (Agentic AI Foundation).
goose is being developed as an open-source software project.

# Extensions

Extensions provide additional tools and context from different data sources and applications.
You can dynamically enable or disable extensions as needed to help complete tasks.

Because you dynamically load extensions, your conversation history may refer
to interactions with extensions that are not currently active. The currently
active extensions are below. Each of these extensions provides tools that are
in your tool specification.


## analyze

### Instructions
Analyze code structure using tree-sitter AST parsing. Three auto-selected modes:
- Directory path → structure overview (file tree with function/class counts)
- File path → semantic details (functions, classes, imports, call counts)
- Any path + focus parameter → symbol call graph (incoming/outgoing chains)

For large codebases, delegate analysis to a subagent and retain only the summary.

## apps

apps supports resources.
### Instructions
Use this extension to create, manage, and iterate on custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript apps.
## developer

### Instructions
Use the developer extension to build software and operate a terminal.

Make sure to use the tools *efficiently* - reading all the content you need in as few
iterations as possible and then making the requested edits or running commands. You are
responsible for managing your context window, and to minimize unnecessary turns which
cost the user money.

For editing software, prefer the flow of using tree to understand the codebase structure
and file sizes. When you need to search, prefer rg which correctly respects gitignored
content. Then use cat or sed to gather the context you need, always reading before editing.
Use write and edit to efficiently make changes. Test and verify as appropriate.

## extensionmanager

### Instructions
Extension Management

Use these tools to discover, enable, and disable extensions, as well as review resources.

Available tools:
- search_available_extensions: Find extensions available to enable/disable
- manage_extensions: Enable or disable extensions
- list_resources: List resources from extensions
- read_resource: Read specific resources from extensions

When you lack the tools needed to complete a task, use search_available_extensions first
to discover what extensions can help.

Use manage_extensions to enable or disable specific extensions by name.
Use list_resources and read_resource to work with extension data and resources.

## skills

### Instructions


You have these skills at your disposal, when it is clear they can help you solve a problem or you are asked to use them:
• goose-doc-guide - Reference goose documentation to create, configure, or explain goose-specific features like recipes, extensions, sessions, and providers. You MUST fetch relevant goose docs before answering. You MUST NOT rely on training data or assumptions for any goose-specific fields, values, names, syntax, or commands.
## summon


## todo

### Instructions
Your todo content is automatically available in your context.

Workflow:
- Start: write initial checklist
- During: update progress
- End: verify all complete

Template:
- [x] Requirement 1
- [ ] Task
  - [ ] Sub-task
- [ ] Requirement 2
- [ ] Another task

[{"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "text", "content": ""}], "tool_calls": [{"id": "call_pIqyNZDrbAFBCXfAh3jBx4mG", "type": "function", "function": {"name": "shell", "arguments": "{\"command\":\"python3 - <<'PY'\\nimport struct,zlib\\npath='/app/image_1.png'\\nwith open(path,'rb') as f:\\n    assert f.read(8)==b'\\\\x89PNG\\\\r\\\\n\\\\x1a\\\\n'\\n    chunks=[]\\n    while True:\\n        b=f.read(8)\\n        if not b: break\\n        length,ctype=struct.unpack('>I4s',b)\\n        data=f.read(length); f.read(4)\\n        chunks.append((ctype,data))\\n        if ctype==b'IEND': break\\n\\nihdr=dict()\\nfor c,d in chunks:\\n    if c==b'IHDR':\\n        w,h,bit_depth,color_type,comp,flt,inter=struct.unpack('>IIBBBBB',d)\\n        print('IHDR',w,h,bit_depth,color_type,comp,flt,inter)\\n    if c==b'IDAT':\\n        pass\\nidat=b''.join(d for c,d in chunks if c==b'IDAT')\\nraw=zlib.decompress(idat)\\nprint('raw len',len(raw))\\n# bytes per pixel\\nchannels={0:1,2:3,3:1,4:2,6:4}[color_type]\\nbpp=max(1,(channels*bit_depth+7)//8)\\nstride=((channels*bit_depth*w+7)//8)\\nprint('bpp',bpp,'stride',stride)\\n# unfilter\\nout=[]\\nprev=bytearray(stride)\\ni=0\\nfor y in range(h):\\n    ftype=raw[i]; i+=1\\n    scan=bytearray(raw[i:i+stride]); i+=stride\\n    recon=bytearray(stride)\\n    if ftype==0:\\n        recon=scan\\n    elif ftype==1:\\n        for x in range(stride):\\n            left=recon[x-bpp] if x>=bpp else 0\\n            recon[x]=(scan[x]+left)&255\\n    elif ftype==2:\\n        for x in range(stride):\\n            up=prev[x]\\n            recon[x]=(scan[x]+up)&255\\n    elif ftype==3:\\n        for x in range(stride):\\n            left=recon[x-bpp] if x>=bpp else 0\\n            up=prev[x]\\n            recon[x]=(scan[x]+((left+up)//2))&255\\n    elif ftype==4:\\n        def paeth(a,b,c):\\n            p=a+b-c\\n            pa=abs(p-a); pb=abs(p-b); pc=abs(p-c)\\n            return a if pa<=pb and pa<=pc else b if pb<=pc else c\\n        for x in range(stride):\\n            a=recon[x-bpp] if x>=bpp else 0\\n            b=prev[x]\\n            c=prev[x-bpp] if x>=bpp else 0\\n            recon[x]=(scan[x]+paeth(a,b,c))&255\\n    else:\\n        raise SystemExit('filter '+str(ftype))\\n    out.append(recon)\\n    prev=recon\\nprint('decoded rows',len(out), 'first bytes', out[0][:20])\\n# sample grayscale ascii of every 8 rows/8 cols from full image\\n# assume 8-bit rgb/rgba\\nfrom math import floor\\nchars='@%#*+=-:. '\\n# crop upper half maybe map exists there\\nfor y0 in [0,40,80,120,160,200,240]:\\n    print('\\\\nY',y0)\\n    for y in range(y0,min(y0+40,h),4):\\n        line=''\\n        for x in range(0,w,8):\\n            idx=x*channels\\n            px=out[y][idx:idx+channels]\\n            if color_type==6:\\n                r,g,b,a=px\\n                if a==0: r=g=b=255\\n            elif color_type==2:\\n                r,g,b=px\\n            elif color_type==0:\\n                r=g=b=px[0]\\n            else:\\n                r=g=b=px[0]\\n            lum=(int(r)+int(g)+int(b))//3\\n            line+=chars[min(len(chars)-1, lum*len(chars)//256)]\\n        print(line)\\nPY\",\"timeout_secs\":20}"}}], "finish_reason": "stop"}]
